def: The first language a person learns from birth.
Liked or enjoyed by many people.
A warm greeting used to welcome someone.
A polite phrase said when meeting someone for the first time.
Said to wish someone an enjoyable time.
A specific food that is prepared and served as part of a meal.
A plastic card used to pay for things by borrowing money.
Used to say that something exists or is available.
New and up to date in style or design.
A thick liquid used for cooking, machines, or fuel.
A product sold at a reduced price for a limited time.
Used to describe sunny weather.
Used to describe rainy weather.
Used to describe snowy weather.
Used to describe cold temperature or weather.
A talk given by a professor to teach university students.
Used to express mild disappointment about something.
Used to apologize or express regret.
A person whose job is acting in films, theater, or television.
To walk for a long time outdoors, especially in hills or mountains.
To fix something that is broken or not working.
To give medical care to a person or an illness.
Asks what topic is currently being discussed.
Used to say you can sleep late without needing to get up.
To be in good physical shape and health.
To go and watch a play or performance at a theater.
To listen to songs or music for enjoyment.
To browse websites on the internet, often for fun.
To play music on the piano.
To play the board game chess.
A board where notices and announcements are posted.
Used to say you like doing something, or would prefer another option.
Used to state the amount of rent that must be paid.
Said to end a phone call politely.
A long piece of fabric worn around the neck for warmth.
To separate items into groups, for example by color.
To pour or put a substance into a container.
A person trained to design, build, or maintain machines and structures.
A person who creates art, such as paintings, music, or writing.
Not difficult; simple to do or understand.
A greeting used when meeting someone in the morning.
A polite greeting used during the day.
A greeting used when meeting someone in the evening.
A polite way to say goodbye when leaving.
